app.name: SiriusPad
app.tagline: Scratchpad for developers. Fast, dark, and local.

sidebar.workspaceHint: Create more workspaces with the + button.

note.addTagHelp: Add a tag and press Enter to confirm.
note.deleteDirtyDescription: This note has unsaved changes. They will be saved before moving it to the trash.
note.closeDirtyDescription: Do you want to save before closing it?
note.closeWindowDescription: The active note still has unsaved changes.
note.noActiveDescription: Create a note from the sidebar or use Ctrl+N to start writing.

workspace.name: Workspace name
workspace.deleteConfirm: Delete workspace "{{name}}" and move its notes to "{{fallback}}"?

gist.publicConfirm: Choose how you want to create this Gist.
gist.tokenRequired: A GitHub token is required to export a Gist.

commands.copyAsCode: Copy note as fenced code block

editor.title: Editor
editor.description: Font, wrapping, autosave, and editor ergonomics.

appearance.title: Appearance
appearance.description: Interface zoom, dark-only theme, and the font used across the UI.

shortcuts.title: Shortcuts
shortcuts.description: Local shortcuts active inside the app. Global palette is Ctrl+Shift+K.

variables.title: Global Variables
variables.description: Substituted in snippets, copies, and exports.

integrations.title: Integrations
integrations.description: GitHub Gist export uses a token with the gist scope.

fields.themeLocked: SiriusPad is dark-only. Use this section to adjust interface scale and typography.
fields.uiZoomHint: Scales the whole app, similar to browser zoom.

searchPanel.placeholder: Type a command or search notes...
searchPanel.commandHelp: Quick actions, note navigation, and app controls in one place.

updater.description: A new version is ready to install.

terminal.title: Terminal
terminal.subtitle: Local shell without leaving the note.
terminal.ready: Shell ready. Type a command and press Enter.
terminal.processRunning: There is already a process running in the terminal.
terminal.runHint: Enter runs. Ctrl+C cancels the current process.
terminal.unsupportedHint: Output appears here when the note language supports execution.

welcome.title: Welcome to SiriusPad
welcome.description: SiriusPad is built to keep bugs, ideas, snippets, commands, and technical notes nearby without slowing your flow.

notes.title: Fast notes, still organized
notes.description: Each note combines title, Markdown, tags, priority, color, and workspace. It works as a personal backlog, debug pad, or snippet base.

tags.title: Read context at a glance
tags.description: Colored tags and priority make it easy to spot whether something is a bug, urgent item, idea, or already done.

terminal.title: A real embedded terminal
terminal.description: Open it with Ctrl+`. Type commands directly, browse history with arrow keys, stop running tasks with Ctrl+C, and send note blocks to the terminal.

command.title: Command palette, made clear
command.description: Ctrl+K opens the app command center. Type an action, note name, or workspace; use arrow keys to navigate and Enter to run.

shortcuts.title: Shortcuts worth memorizing
shortcuts.description: These are the ones that speed up everyday use the most. You do not need all of them now, but they stay visible in the UI.

ready.title: Ready to go
ready.description: If you forget something, you can reopen the tutorial from settings. The goal is to be quick to start and comfortable to keep using.

visuals.notesBody: Fix updater flow and review window shortcuts
visuals.welcomeBadge: A dark, local, no-nonsense pad for people who live between code, terminal, and checklists.
visuals.welcomePoint1: Markdown for writing fast without losing structure.
visuals.welcomePoint2: Workspaces to separate context without clutter.
visuals.welcomePoint3: Terminal, history, and shortcuts wired into the flow.
visuals.readyBadge: Everything stays local and the app opens ready to continue.
visuals.readyHint: Start with Ctrl+N, use Ctrl+K when you do not remember a command, and open the terminal with Ctrl+` when you need to run something.
